reasoning: The Lean statement captures the informal claim exactly. "Finite abelian group" is encoded by `CommGroup G` + `Fintype G`. The hypothesis `n ∣ card G` together with the fact that a group always has `card G ≥ 1` (nonempty due to the identity) automatically forces `n > 0`, so "positive divisor" is correctly captured without needing an explicit `n > 0` hypothesis. The conclusion `∃ (H : Subgroup G) (H_fin : Fintype H), @card H H_fin = n` is the standard way of asserting "there exists a subgroup of order n" — the explicit `Fintype H` is stylistic and logically equivalent to the inferable instance. Quantifier structure ("for each divisor" → universal in n, "has a subgroup of order n" → existence) matches. No hypotheses are dropped or added beyond standard mathematical reading.
